mission.title: caddy: Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive
mission.description: Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive. This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant caddy resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Inside of a `tls` block you can put the `resolvers` directive to specify the resolvers used for DNS ACME challenge. With the new global `dns` setting, the `resolvers` setting should also have a global

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive
steps.description:
- Added `tls_resolvers` global option that accepts one or more DNS resolver addresses
- Global resolvers are stored in the TLS app configuration
- Resolvers are automatically applied to ACME issuers that use DNS challenges
- Local `resolvers` directive in `tls` blocks can override global settings

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Allow global DNS `resolvers` directive is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: - Added `tls_resolvers` global option that accepts one or more DNS resolver addresses
- Global resolvers are stored in the TLS app configuration
- Resolvers are automatically applied to ACME issuers that use DNS challenges
- Local `resolvers` directive in `tls` blocks can override global settings. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
